message:
fix(cass): correct token estimation for whitespace-only splits and harden export session resolution

Two fixes in the cass module:

1. estimate_text_tokens: The previous logic assigned the split_whitespace
   count to a variable and then checked if it was > 0, but this
   incorrectly returned 0 for strings containing only non-whitespace
   characters (the count was 1, not 0, for a single word). Replaced with
   a simpler check: if the trimmed string contains any whitespace, use
   split_whitespace().count(); otherwise fall back to the char/4
   heuristic. This also avoids an unnecessary intermediate variable.

2. resolve_export_session: When resolving by synthetic row-ID identifier
   (e.g. "ft-session-42"), the function now verifies that the session
   found at that row ID actually produces the same export identifier.
   This prevents a subtle bug where a session with an explicit session_id
   (which changes its export identifier) could be incorrectly returned
   when looked up by its row-ID-based synthetic identifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
